title: LogRecord
description: Structured log line emitted by `services/shared/logger.ts`. Every record produced by `services/signaling/`, `services/ai-gateway/`, `services/turn/`, or any future backend service MUST validate against this shape before emission. The `redactedFields` allow-list is the only place free-form fields may live; everything else is closed and per-channel.

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at log emission. Wall-clock; never used for gameplay determinism. The logger sets this; callers do not.

severity.description: Closed severity ladder. `fatal` is reserved for unrecoverable startup / config failure. `error` carries an `errorId` referenced by the player-facing surface.

channel.description: Closed log channel. `app` = developer runtime; `access` = inbound HTTP / WebSocket; `audit` = consent / wipe / policy events; `security` = SecurityEvent records. Retention + access policy differs per channel; see `docs/operations/log-retention.md` and `docs/operations/access-control.md`.

service.description: Emitting service name; `signaling`, `ai-gateway`, `turn`. Pinned by the logger `base.service`.

correlationId.description: UUID v4 generated at the entry point of each request / WebSocket connection / worker message. Carried through downstream emissions so a single trace stitches across services.

event.description: Snake-case event name; `room_created`, `signature_failure`, `request_completed`. Closed per service via the service-side enum.

fields.description: Structured payload. The logger redact-pass strips any field name listed in the central deny-list (`req.headers.authorization`, `req.body.prompt`, `req.body.theme`, `metadata.playerName`, `apiKey`, `token`, `password`) before emission. Free-form strings are capped at 256 chars to bound log volume.

errorId.description: Optional. Set when the record is correlated with a thrown error so the player-facing toast and the log line carry the same id.

caseId.description: Optional. Player-side report case id (256-bit random hex) per `case-id.schema.json`. Tags every record for the reported session so the maintainer can fetch the trace from a single id with no PII.
